content.text: Let me verify both edits look correct in context:

Both edits are correct and in place. Here's a summary of what was added to `common/chat.cpp`:

---

### Changes made to `common/chat.cpp`

**1. New handler function** (lines 1624–1660), inserted before `common_chat_extra_context()`:

```cpp
static common_chat_params common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(
        const common_chat_template &          tmpl,
        const autoparser::generation_params & inputs) {
```

The handler iterates `inputs.messages` and builds `data.prompt` manually:
- **System role** — stored and prepended (with a `\n`) to the next user message, then cleared
- **User role** — first turn opens with `<s>[INST] `, subsequent turns just `[INST] `; closes with ` [/INST]`
- **Assistant role** — appended as ` {content}</s>`
- Sets `data.format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY`

This produces the correct Mistral v0.3 format, e.g.:
- With system: `<s>[INST] {system}\n{user1} [/INST] {assistant1}</s>[INST] {user2} [/INST]`
- Without system: `<s>[INST] {user1} [/INST] {assistant1}</s>[INST] {user2} [/INST]`

**2. Detection block** (lines 1728–1735), inserted at the end of `common_chat_try_specialized_template()` before the final `return std::nullopt`:

```cpp
if (src.find("[INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[/INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```

The negative `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` check ensures the existing Ministral 3 handler (which uses `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`) isn't accidentally matched. No existing code was modified.

Both edits are correct and in place. Here's a summary of what was added to `common/chat.cpp`:

---

### Changes made to `common/chat.cpp`

**1. New handler function** (lines 1624–1660), inserted before `common_chat_extra_context()`:

```cpp
static common_chat_params common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(
        const common_chat_template &          tmpl,
        const autoparser::generation_params & inputs) {
```

The handler iterates `inputs.messages` and builds `data.prompt` manually:
- **System role** — stored and prepended (with a `\n`) to the next user message, then cleared
- **User role** — first turn opens with `<s>[INST] `, subsequent turns just `[INST] `; closes with ` [/INST]`
- **Assistant role** — appended as ` {content}</s>`
- Sets `data.format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY`

This produces the correct Mistral v0.3 format, e.g.:
- With system: `<s>[INST] {system}\n{user1} [/INST] {assistant1}</s>[INST] {user2} [/INST]`
- Without system: `<s>[INST] {user1} [/INST] {assistant1}</s>[INST] {user2} [/INST]`

**2. Detection block** (lines 1728–1735), inserted at the end of `common_chat_try_specialized_template()` before the final `return std::nullopt`:

```cpp
if (src.find("[INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[/INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```

The negative `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` check ensures the existing Ministral 3 handler (which uses `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`) isn't accidentally matched. No existing code was modified.
